channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mayor: Family was to celebrate child's birthday when NYC sightseeing helicopter crashed, killing 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A family from Spain was about to celebrate the ninth birthday of one of their children when their sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey, killing all six people aboard in the latest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Finger-biting liquor thief in Kansas still on the run: Police
item.description: Lawrence Police continue to search for the man who tore off the tip of a person's finger with his teeth before getting away.
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump will undergo his annual physical Friday after years of reluctance to share medical information
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump is undergoing his annual physical on Friday, potentially giving the public its first details in years about the health of a man who in January became the oldest in U.S. history to be sworn in as president.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration to refer Maine to Justice Department over transgender participation in sports
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The deadline arrived Friday for Maine officials to reach a resolution with the U.S. Education Department over a finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing transgender athletes to participate in girls’ sports.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Near record heat builds across Utah ahead of cooldown
item.description: The heat is on across the Beehive State for the next few days with cooler weather forecasted to set in Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Alana Brophy, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey woman identified as one of 200 Dominican Republic nightclub collapse victims
item.description: Family and friends of a New Jersey woman identified her as one of the more than 200 people who died in a nightclub collapse in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USPS warns about common scams
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service and Postal Inspection Service want to warn consumers to be aware of fraud and spam regarding their mail, and what to do to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: New York bills would protect Tesla owners, target Tesla dominance
item.description: That measure follows nationwide reports of Tesla owners, dealerships, and charging stations being vandalized.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominee for public lands post withdraws after her criticism of Jan. 6 attack surfaces
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to oversee an agency that manages a quarter-billion acres of public land has withdrawn her nomination following revelations that she criticized the Republican president in 2021 for inciting the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina woman wanted in murder investigation found in Netherlands
item.description: Dutch police have located a South Carolina woman, who is wanted in the murder of her ex-boyfriend and the father of her child, in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Six people have died in a helicopter crash in the Hudson River in New York City, an AP source says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six people have died in a helicopter crash in the Hudson River in New York City, an Associated Press source says. THIS IS A BREAKING NEWS UPDATE. AP’s earlier story follows below.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 4 West Virginia University students, 3 alumni have visas revoked by US government
item.description: WVU's Director Johnson said that in every case, the government's justification was that the person's name was "found in a criminal records check.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, NewsNation

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: “In his short time in government, Elon Musk has done enormous harm to working Americans,” House Democrats said.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ moves to drop charges against man it labeled MS-13 leader
item.description: The move to dismiss the charges prompted the unusual push from Villatoro Santos’s lawyer seeking to delay the dropping of any charges against his client.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Students react to nearly 50 international students' visas being revoked, records being removed across Utah
item.description: International students at institutions across Utah and around the country are having their records terminated or their visas revoked. Here's what some students said about it.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: 1,700 pounds of butter recalled over bacteria contamination
item.description: Nearly a ton of salted butter is being recalled because, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the product is "contaminated with elevated levels of coliform," a bacteria found in animals' digestive tracts and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp to 78 cents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is seeking a rate increase this summer that includes hiking the cost of a first-class stamp from 73 cents to 78 cents. The request was made Wednesday to the Postal Regulatory Commission, which must OK the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Universal Studios announces plans to build first European theme park resort
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences officially announced on Tuesday that it intends to build its first-ever theme park and resort in the United Kingdom. In 2023, Universal purchased a 476-acre parcel of land outside Bedford, England, about 55 miles north of London.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: Two stowaways found dead in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue plane earlier this year have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Are dire wolves de-extinct? Genetically engineered pups spark controversy
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Teen seen shoving guns through border wall into Mexico
item.description: An Arizona teen allegedly was caught slipping guns and knives through the border wall south into Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5

item.title: Rich on Tech Radio Recap: Nintendo News, Mesh Wi-Fi & Online Privacy
item.description: You see my segments on TV, but did you know I also host a national radio show called Rich On Tech? Each week, I share helpful tech news, tips, and answer your questions on radio stations across the country — from KFI AM 640 in Los Angeles to 710 WOR in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft fires employees who protested its support for Israel at 50th anniversary event
item.description: Two Microsoft employees were fired for protests condemning the company’s support for Israel at a 50th anniversary event last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Salt Lake Bees' new stadium in Daybreak expected to bring economic impacts, growth to local businesses
item.description: South Jordan is home to local businesses and neighbors who say they're starting to feel the effects of increased foot traffic from a new baseball stadium.
item.dc:creator: McKenzie Diaz, ABC 4

item.title: International students at several Utah universities had their visas revoked
item.description: On Tuesday, a spokesperson for the University of Utah confirmed that about 17 international students or graduates have had their visas revoked as of April 8.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Agreement in works to settle Northwestern hazing lawsuits that led to football coach's firing
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former Northwestern University football players are finalizing an agreement with the school to settle lawsuits alleging hazing and abuse that led to longtime coach Pat Fitzgerald’s firing, a school spokesman and attorneys said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5
